main.description: A signalled transition on a goal (TASK-84). Published as a message on msg.topic.goals — an observation that goal <goal> moved, NOT the goal's current value. Any agent or human may emit one. It SIGNALS; it does not manage (ADR-0035): the current value lives in the goal.<id> artifact (single-writer by convention); the owner and operator stay authoritative; a goal.update reports, it never assigns, gates, or directs an agent. The goal's status is derived from its criteria, so an update headlines a movement (a criterion met, a goal opened) rather than stamping a state enum. A goal.update for a goal with no artifact yet is a proposal to open one. Content is opaque to the bus. This is the same current-value-artifact + observable-event-stream pairing the workflow harness uses (its run artifact alongside msg.workflow.<id>.events).

goal.description: The goal-id this update concerns (matches the goal.<id> artifact).

crit.description: Optional criterion id this update concerns, when the movement is about one acceptance criterion rather than the whole goal.

status.description: Optional new status this update reports. For a criterion (crit set): met | in-progress | waiting-on-you | blocked | not-started. Omitted for a whole-goal observation whose status is just derived from the criteria.

headline.description: Short line describing the transition / what was observed, e.g. 'lexicons merged -> criterion 1 met' or 'goal opened: ship v0.5'.

ref.description: Optional artifact/PR/ticket that triggered the update (e.g. the proof artifact backing a met criterion). Goals are generic — a ref MAY point at a ticket, but the primitive never requires one.

by.description: Who/what signalled it (convenience label; the bus-stamped author is authoritative).
